title: FHIR API Error Rate
spl:
index=healthcare sourcetype IN ("apigee:fhir","hapi:fhir")
| eval err=if(status>=400,1,0)
| timechart span=5m sum(err) as errors, count as reqs
| eval err_pct=round(errors/reqs*100,2)
| where err_pct > 2
description: High 4xx/5xx on FHIR hurts patient apps and partner integrations (ONC interoperability).
value: High 4xx/5xx on FHIR hurts patient apps and partner integrations (ONC interoperability).
implementation: Break out by client_id and resource; rate-limit abusive partners; SLO dashboards for Patient/$everything bulk exports.
visualization: Line chart (error %), Bar chart (errors by route), Table (top clients).
knownFalsePositives: FHIR error bursts when EHR vendors deploy app versions, public APIs get throttled, or mobile clients retry; compare to version adoption curves.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: API gateway, EHR FHIR logs.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="apigee:fhir"`, `sourcetype="hapi:fhir"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Break out by client_id and resource; rate-limit abusive partners; SLO dashboards for Patient/$everything bulk exports.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=healthcare sourcetype IN ("apigee:fhir","hapi:fhir")
| eval err=if(status>=400,1,0)
| timechart span=5m sum(err) as errors, count as reqs
| eval err_pct=round(errors/reqs*100,2)
| where err_pct > 2
```

Understanding this SPL

**FHIR API Error Rate** — High 4xx/5xx on FHIR hurts patient apps and partner integrations (ONC interoperability).

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="apigee:fhir"`, `sourcetype="hapi:fhir"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): API gateway, EHR FHIR logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: healthcare.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=healthcare. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **err** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=5m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **err_pct** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where err_pct > 2` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (error %), Bar chart (errors by route), Table (top clients).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "FHIR API Error Rate" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
